headline: MACC's prevention division received only 6% of total budget
context: MACC allocated only 6% of its RM380 million budget to corruption prevention programmes in 2025, with 71% going to investigation and enforcement operations.

cards.big: MACC spends 6% on prevention, 71% on investigation — treating symptoms over causes
cards.sub: Anti-corruption strategy prioritises catching corrupt officials over stopping corruption

cards.big: UNCAC Article 6 calls for dedicated preventive anti-corruption bodies
cards.sub: Malaysia ratified UNCAC in 2008 but prevention remains severely underfunded

cards.big: Prevention programmes cost RM22.8 million — each investigation costs RM180,000 on average
cards.sub: One prevention programme can eliminate need for hundreds of investigations

cards.big: Singapore's CPIB allocates 25% of budget to prevention and public education
cards.sub: Effective anti-corruption models invest heavily upstream, not just downstream

cards.big: Catching corrupt people is necessary but preventing corruption is cheaper and more effective
Should MACC be required to spend at least 20% of its budget on prevention?
